value.original: Allows to configure the date format and decimal separator for a CSV importer

row.value: Allows to configure the date format and decimal separator for a CSV importer

value.original: Date Format for the CSV importer

row.value: Date Format for the CSV importer

value.original: Define the date format for dates that is used in the CSV file. Common examples are: dd/MM/yyyy for days/month/year (31/12/2050) or MM/dd/yyyy for months/days/year (12/31/2050). For more information, visit http://docs.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html

row.value: Define the date format for dates that is used in the CSV file. Common examples are: dd/MM/yyyy for days/month/year (31/12/2050) or MM/dd/yyyy for months/days/year (12/31/2050). For more information, visit http://docs.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html

value.original: Decimal Separator for the CSV importer

row.value: Decimal Separator for the CSV importer

value.original: Define the decimal separator for amounts that is used in the CSV file. Common values are . (dot) or , (comma). Example: the decimal separator for 123,45 is a comma (,)

row.value: Define the decimal separator for amounts that is used in the CSV file. Common values are . (dot) or , (comma). Example: the decimal separator for 123,45 is a comma (,)

value.original: A delimiter is a sequence of one character used to specify the boundary between separate, independent regions in plain text or other data streams.

row.value: A delimiter is a sequence of one character used to specify the boundary between separate, independent regions in plain text or other data streams.

value.original: An example of a delimiter is the comma character, which acts as a field delimiter in a sequence of comma-separated values. The most common delimiters are the comma, tab, and colon.

row.value: An example of a delimiter is the comma character, which acts as a field delimiter in a sequence of comma-separated values. The most common delimiters are the comma, tab, and colon.
